---
title: "nitrix.stats.basis"
generated: true
signature-hash: 95195408f53ab53f
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.basis`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `g` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
| `K` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
| `q` | random-effect columns |
| `D` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `R` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `SmoothBasis` {#SmoothBasis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SmoothBasis -->
```python
class SmoothBasis(Protocol)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:SmoothBasis -->
Structural interface of a GAM smooth term (audit D8).

A smooth carries a `design` matrix and a coefficient count `dim`, knows
its own penalty blocks, and can re-evaluate its design on a covariate grid.
`gam_fit` dispatches through these members instead of an `isinstance`
chain, so a **new** basis type (implementing this Protocol) needs no edit to
`gam.py` -- the open-set registry the :class:`Family` / :class:`CorrSpec`
surfaces already have.

The concrete bases (:class:`SplineBasis` / :class:`TensorBasis` /
:class:`REBasis`) all conform.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SmoothBasis.penalty_blocks -->
```python
penalty_blocks() -> List[Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Penalty blocks `[(S_block, eig_block)]` (host constants).

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SmoothBasis.eval_design -->
```python
eval_design(x: Any) -> Float[Array, 'g k']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Re-evaluate the design on the smooth's covariate grid `x`.

## `SplineBasis` {#SplineBasis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SplineBasis -->
```python
class SplineBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:SplineBasis -->
A penalised spline smooth: design, penalty, and re-evaluation params.

The re-evaluation at new covariate values factors as `design(x) =
raw_features(x) @ constraint` for every basis `kind`: only the
(nonlinear) `raw_features` differs -- B-spline taps for `'bspline'`, the
radial + polynomial features for `'tprs'` (thin-plate).  `constraint` is
the shared sum-to-zero reparameterisation.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SplineBasis.penalty_blocks -->
```python
penalty_blocks() -> list
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Penalty blocks `[(S, eig)]` (audit D8): the `(k, k)` roughness
penalty and its eigenvalues, the unpenalised null space floored to
zero.  `eig` drives only the basis-invariant `tr(S_lambda^+ S_k)`.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SplineBasis.eval_design -->
```python
eval_design(x: Float[Array, ' g']) -> Float[Array, 'g k']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Re-evaluate the spline design on a covariate grid `x` (audit D8).

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SplineBasis.tree_flatten -->
```python
tree_flatten() -> Tuple[Tuple[Any, ...], Tuple[str, int, int, int, float, float, Optional[float]]]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SplineBasis.tree_unflatten -->
```python
tree_unflatten(aux: Tuple[str, int, int, int, float, float, Optional[float]], children: Tuple[Any, ...]) -> 'SplineBasis'
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SplineBasis.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(design: Float[Array, 'n k'], penalty: Float[Array, 'k k'], kind: str, constraint: Optional[Float[Array, 'k0 k']], n_basis: int, degree: int, penalty_order: int, lo: float, hi: float, knots: Optional[Float[Array, ' nk']] = None, radial_transform: Optional[Float[Array, 'nk kw']] = None, kernel_param: Optional[float] = None) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `TensorBasis` {#TensorBasis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TensorBasis -->
```python
class TensorBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:TensorBasis -->
An anisotropic tensor-product interaction smooth `f(x_1, ..., x_d)`.

Built from `d` marginal :class:`SplineBasis` (already sum-to-zero
constrained) by the **row-wise tensor product** of their designs and the
Kronecker-sum of per-margin penalties `S_j = I (x) ... P_j ... (x) I` --
so each margin keeps its own smoothing parameter (selected jointly by the
GAM Fellner-Schall loop, which handles `> 1` penalty per smooth).  This is
mgcv's tensor **interaction** (`ti`): the marginal centring removes the
main effects, so the term models the *interaction* and stays identifiable
against the intercept; add marginal `s(x_j)` smooths for a full `te`.

The marginal penalties commute (Kronecker structure), so they are
**simultaneously diagonalisable**: `pen_eig[j]` is penalty `j` in the
joint eigenbasis (`U_1 (x) ... (x) U_d`), which the GAM engine uses to
evaluate the smoothing-parameter trace `tr(S_lambda^+ S_j)` as an
elementwise sum -- cuSOLVER-free, no per-iteration pseudo-inverse -- while
the fit itself keeps the original (non-rotated) tensor basis.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TensorBasis.penalty_blocks -->
```python
penalty_blocks() -> list
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Penalty blocks `[(S_j, eig_j)]` (audit D8): one per margin -- the
Kronecker-embedded penalty and its joint-eigenbasis eigenvalues.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TensorBasis.eval_design -->
```python
eval_design(x: Tuple[Float[Array, ' g'], ...]) -> Float[Array, 'g K']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Re-evaluate the tensor design on matched per-margin grids `x`
(audit D8): pass one length-`g` grid per margin.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TensorBasis.tree_flatten -->
```python
tree_flatten() -> Tuple[Tuple[Any, ...], Tuple[int, ...]]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TensorBasis.tree_unflatten -->
```python
tree_unflatten(aux: Tuple[int, ...], children: Tuple[Any, ...]) -> 'TensorBasis'
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TensorBasis.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(design: Float[Array, 'n K'], penalties: Float[Array, 'd K K'], pen_eig: Float[Array, 'd K'], margins: Tuple[SplineBasis, ...], dims: Tuple[int, ...]) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `REBasis` {#REBasis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:REBasis -->
```python
class REBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:REBasis -->
A random-effect smooth block (mgcv `bs="re"`) for a GAMM.

A random intercept / slope per level of a grouping factor, expressed as the
`(design, penalty)` block the GAM penalty machinery already consumes: the
design is the one-hot indicator (intercept) or one-hot times a covariate
(slope) over the factor levels, and the penalty is the **identity** (a
ridge).  The single smoothing parameter the Fellner-Schall loop selects
**is** the random-effect precision `lambda = 1 / sigma_b^2` -- the v1
"penalised GLM == variance-components REML" identity, now reachable.  It is a
third :data:`Smooth` variant alongside :class:`SplineBasis` /
:class:`TensorBasis`, so it slots straight into `gam_fit` with **no new
solver work** (a random effect is just one more penalty block).

Unlike a spline, an RE has no continuous covariate to re-evaluate, so it
carries only `(design, penalty, levels)` -- not the spline construction
parameters.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:REBasis.penalty_blocks -->
```python
penalty_blocks() -> list
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Penalty blocks `[(I, 1)]` (audit D8): the identity ridge (full rank
`q`, eigenvalues exactly one -- no host eigh).

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:REBasis.eval_design -->
```python
eval_design(x: Float[Array, ' g']) -> Float[Array, 'g q']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Re-evaluate as the one-hot of the requested level indices `x`
(audit D8): the per-level random effect at those levels.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:REBasis.tree_flatten -->
```python
tree_flatten() -> Tuple[Tuple[Array, ...], Tuple[int]]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:REBasis.tree_unflatten -->
```python
tree_unflatten(aux: Tuple[int], children: Tuple[Any, ...]) -> 'REBasis'
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:REBasis.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(design: Float[Array, 'n q'], penalty: Float[Array, 'q q'], levels: int) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `bspline_basis` {#bspline_basis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:bspline_basis -->
```python
bspline_basis(x: Float[Array, ' n'], n_basis: int = 10, *, degree: int = 3, penalty_order: int = 2, bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, center: bool = True) -> SplineBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:bspline_basis -->
Build a P-spline smooth basis for covariate `x`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:bspline_basis -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `x` | `Float` | `n` | — | `(n,)` covariate values. |
| `n_basis` | `int` | — | `10` | Number of B-spline basis functions `k` (`mgcv`'s `k`; default `10`). |
| `degree` | `int` | — | `3` | B-spline degree (default `3`, cubic). |
| `penalty_order` | `int` | — | `2` | Order of the difference penalty (default `2` -- penalise curvature, the standard P-spline). |
| `bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | `(lo, hi)` knot range. Defaults to a small margin around the data range so the boundary knots are not exactly at the extreme points. |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `True` | Apply the sum-to-zero identifiability constraint (default `True`); the returned basis then has `k - 1` columns. |

**Returns** `SplineBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `cyclic_cubic_basis` {#cyclic_cubic_basis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:cyclic_cubic_basis -->
```python
cyclic_cubic_basis(x: Float[Array, ' n'], n_basis: int = 10, *, degree: int = 3, penalty_order: int = 2, bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, center: bool = True) -> SplineBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:cyclic_cubic_basis -->
Build a cyclic cubic P-spline basis for a periodic covariate `x`.

For periodic covariates (cortical angle, phase, time-of-day): a uniform
cyclic B-spline design (the basis wraps at the period ends, matching `f`,
`f'`, `f''`) with a circular difference penalty (`mgcv`'s `bs='cp'`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:cyclic_cubic_basis -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `x` | `Float` | `n` | — | `(n,)` covariate values. |
| `n_basis` | `int` | — | `10` | Number of cyclic basis functions `k`. |
| `degree` | `int` | — | `3` | B-spline degree (default `3`) and circular difference order (`2`). |
| `penalty_order` | `int` | — | `2` | B-spline degree (default `3`) and circular difference order (`2`). |
| `bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | `(lo, hi)` = the period; `hi` is identified with `lo`. Defaults to the data range (pass the true period for a partial-cycle sample). |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `True` | Sum-to-zero identifiability constraint (default `True`). |

**Returns** `SplineBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `thinplate_regression_basis` {#thinplate_regression_basis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:thinplate_regression_basis -->
```python
thinplate_regression_basis(x: Float[Array, ' n'], n_basis: int = 10, *, penalty_order: int = 2, max_knots: int = 100, bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, center: bool = True) -> SplineBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:thinplate_regression_basis -->
Build a thin-plate regression spline (TPRS) basis for covariate `x`.

The `mgcv` default smoother (`bs='tp'`): isotropic and knot-free.  The
full thin-plate basis (one function per knot) is truncated by an
eigendecomposition of the radial penalty matrix `E` -- the `k - M`
leading (largest-eigenvalue) wiggly components, plus the `M = penalty_order`
unpenalised polynomial null-space terms (Wood 2003).  The truncation
`eigh` is a one-off host (CPU/numpy) computation -- it is data-dependent
but not per-trace, and avoids the cuSOLVER `syevd` on the broken GPU.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:thinplate_regression_basis -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `x` | `Float` | `n` | — | `(n,)` covariate values. |
| `n_basis` | `int` | — | `10` | Total basis dimension `k` (wiggly + polynomial; `mgcv`'s `k`). |
| `penalty_order` | `int` | — | `2` | Thin-plate order `m` (default `2` -- penalise curvature); the polynomial null space has dimension `M = m`. |
| `max_knots` | `int` | — | `100` | Cap on the number of knots (placed at quantiles of `x` when `n` exceeds it), bounding the `O(n_knots^3)` eigendecomposition. |
| `bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | `(lo, hi)` for the polynomial rescaling (defaults to the data range). |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `True` | Apply the sum-to-zero identifiability constraint (default `True`). |

**Returns** `SplineBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `cr_basis` {#cr_basis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:cr_basis -->
```python
cr_basis(x: Float[Array, ' n'], n_basis: int = 10, *, bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, center: bool = True) -> SplineBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:cr_basis -->
Build a cubic regression spline (mgcv `bs='cr'`) for covariate `x`.

A natural cubic spline parameterised by its values at `n_basis` knots
(placed at quantiles of `x`), with the exact integrated-curvature penalty.
The cheap knot-based 1-D smoother: `O(k)` design, no eigen-truncation.
Equivalent to `scipy`'s natural `CubicSpline` interpolation at the knots.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:cr_basis -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `x` | `Float` | `n` | — | `(n,)` covariate values. |
| `n_basis` | `int` | — | `10` | Number of knots / basis coefficients `k` (>= 4). |
| `bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | Unused for `cr` (knots come from the data quantiles); accepted for a uniform smooth-constructor signature. |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `True` | Apply the sum-to-zero identifiability constraint (default `True`). |

**Returns** `SplineBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `gp_basis` {#gp_basis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gp_basis -->
```python
gp_basis(x: Float[Array, ' n'], n_basis: int = 10, *, rho: Optional[float] = None, max_knots: int = 100, bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, center: bool = True) -> SplineBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gp_basis -->
Build a Gaussian-process smooth (mgcv `bs='gp'`) for covariate `x`.

A low-rank kriging smooth with a **Matern-3/2** covariance kernel of range
`rho`: the smooth is `f(x) = sum_j C(|x - z_j|) delta_j` over knots `z`
with the RKHS penalty `delta^T C_zz delta` (`C_zz` the kernel Gram at the
knots).  Constructed like the thin-plate basis -- the radial features are
eigen-reparameterised so the penalty is diagonal and truncated to the
`n_basis` leading components (a one-off host `eigh`, cuSOLVER-free on the
broken GPU).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gp_basis -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `x` | `Float` | `n` | — | `(n,)` covariate values. |
| `n_basis` | `int` | — | `10` | Retained basis dimension `k` (the leading kriging components). |
| `rho` | `Optional[float]` | — | `None` | Kernel range (correlation length). Defaults to the data range / 2. |
| `max_knots` | `int` | — | `100` | Cap on the number of knots (quantile-placed) bounding the `eigh` cost. |
| `bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | `(lo, hi)` recorded for re-evaluation (defaults to the data range). |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `True` | Apply the sum-to-zero identifiability constraint (default `True`). |

**Returns** `SplineBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `hsgp_basis` {#hsgp_basis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:hsgp_basis -->
```python
hsgp_basis(x: Float[Array, ' n'], n_basis: int = 20, *, kernel: str = 'matern52', rho: Optional[float] = None, amplitude: float = 1.0, boundary: float = 1.5, bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, center: bool = True) -> SplineBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:hsgp_basis -->
Build a Hilbert-space approximate GP smooth (HSGP) for covariate `x`.

A reduced-rank stationary GP on the bounded domain `[c - L, c + L]`
(`c` the data midrange, `L = boundary * half-range`), expanded in the
Dirichlet-Laplacian eigenfunctions `phi_j(x) = sqrt(1/L) sin(sqrt(lam_j)
(x - c + L))` with `sqrt(lam_j) = j pi / (2 L)`.  The kernel enters
**only** through the spectral density weights `s_j = S_theta(sqrt(lam_j))`
(:func:`~nitrix.linalg.kernel.spectral_density`): the whitened design is
`Psi = [sqrt(s_j) phi_j(x)]` with an **identity penalty**, so the basis is
a drop-in smooth for :func:`gam_fit` and the Fellner-Schall smoothing
parameter `lambda = 1 / sigma_f**2` is the GP amplitude.

Unlike the kriging :func:`gp_basis`, the eigenfunctions are independent of
the kernel hyperparameters, so the lengthscale `rho` enters as a diagonal
reweighting of a fixed design -- the basis for `eigh`-free `rho`
estimation (the standalone `gp_fit`; not this constructor, which fixes
`rho`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:hsgp_basis -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `x` | `Float` | `n` | — | `(n,)` covariate values. |
| `n_basis` | `int` | — | `20` | Number of retained eigenfunctions `m` (the reduced rank). |
| `kernel` | `str` | — | `'matern52'` | Stationary kernel: `'matern12'` / `'matern32'` / `'matern52'` / `'rbf'` (squared-exponential). See :func:`~nitrix.linalg.kernel.spectral_density`. |
| `rho` | `Optional[float]` | — | `None` | Kernel lengthscale. Defaults to the data half-range (a sane fixed value; lengthscale *estimation* is the standalone `gp_fit`). |
| `amplitude` | `float` | — | `1.0` | Kernel amplitude folded into the weights; keep `1.0` when fitting via `gam_fit` (the smoothing parameter carries the amplitude). |
| `boundary` | `float` | — | `1.5` | Domain-extension factor `L / half-range` (`>= 1`; default `1.5`). Larger values reduce boundary bias; small `rho` needs larger `n_basis`. |
| `bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | `(lo, hi)` data range override (defaults to the data min/max). |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `True` | Apply the sum-to-zero identifiability constraint (default `True`). |

**Returns** `SplineBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `hsgp_basis_nd` {#hsgp_basis_nd}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:hsgp_basis_nd -->
```python
hsgp_basis_nd(X: Float[Array, 'n D'], n_basis: Any = 8, *, kernel: str = 'matern52', rho: Any = None, amplitude: float = 1.0, boundary: float = 1.5, center: bool = True) -> _HSGPndBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:hsgp_basis_nd -->
Build a multi-dimensional Hilbert-space GP smooth (tensor-product HSGP).

The `D`-D GP smoother (a spatial smooth, or a smooth interaction of `D`
continuous covariates): the reduced-rank GP on a box domain expanded in the
tensor-product Laplace eigenfunctions.  Either **isotropic** (one lengthscale,
a radial kernel of `||x - x'||`) or **separable / ARD** (a per-dimension
lengthscale, a product kernel) -- selected by `rho`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:hsgp_basis_nd -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `X` | `Float` | `n` × `D` | — | `(n, D)` covariates (`D >= 1` columns). |
| `n_basis` | `Any` | — | `8` | Per-dimension rank: an int (the same `m` for every dimension) or a length-`D` sequence `[m_1, ..., m_D]`. The total rank is the product `M = prod_d m_d` (it grows fast with `D` -- keep `m` modest for `D >= 2`). |
| `kernel` | `str` | — | `'matern52'` | Stationary kernel: `'matern52'` (default) / `'matern32'` / `'matern12'` / `'rbf'`. |
| `rho` | `Any` | — | `None` | Lengthscale. A scalar gives an **isotropic** GP (the `D`-dimensional spectral density of `\|\|w\|\|`); a length-`D` sequence gives a **separable / ARD** GP (the product of per-dimension 1-D densities). `None` (default) is isotropic with `rho` the mean per-dimension half-range. |
| `amplitude` | `float` | — | `1.0` | Kernel amplitude folded into the weights; keep `1.0` for `gam_fit`. |
| `boundary` | `float` | — | `1.5` | Domain-extension factor `L_d / half-range_d` (`>= 1`; default `1.5`). |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `True` | Apply the sum-to-zero identifiability constraint (default `True`). |

**Returns** `_HSGPndBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `gp_factor_smooth` {#gp_factor_smooth}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gp_factor_smooth -->
```python
gp_factor_smooth(x: Float[Array, ' n'], group: Int[Array, ' n'], n_basis: int = 10, *, kernel: str = 'matern52', rho: Optional[float] = None, amplitude: float = 1.0, boundary: float = 1.5, bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, center: bool = False, n_levels: Optional[int] = None) -> _FactorGPBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gp_factor_smooth -->
Build a factor-smooth GP interaction (mgcv `bs="fs"` with a GP marginal).

A separate Hilbert-space GP curve of `x` for each level of `group`, all
sharing **one** smoothing parameter (so the per-group curves are random
deviations with a common amplitude -- partial pooling).  This is the
**fixed-`rho`** building block of the hierarchical GP: drop it into
`gam_fit` alongside a population :func:`hsgp_basis` of the same `rho` (and
the intercept) for the "GS" hierarchical GP.  For *estimated* `rho` and the
full variance-component report, use :func:`~nitrix.stats.hgp.hgp_fit`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gp_factor_smooth -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `x` | `Float` | `n` | — | `(n,)` covariate. |
| `group` | `Int` | `n` | — | `(n,)` integer factor labels `0 .. L-1`. |
| `n_basis` | `int` | — | `10` | Per-group HSGP rank `m` (default `10`; the design is `L` times this wide, so a smaller rank than :func:`hsgp_basis` is usual). |
| `kernel` | `str` | — | `'matern52'` | HSGP marginal parameters (see :func:`hsgp_basis`); `rho` defaults to the data half-range (a fixed value -- lengthscale estimation is `hgp_fit`). |
| `rho` | `Optional[float]` | — | `None` | HSGP marginal parameters (see :func:`hsgp_basis`); `rho` defaults to the data half-range (a fixed value -- lengthscale estimation is `hgp_fit`). |
| `amplitude` | `float` | — | `1.0` | HSGP marginal parameters (see :func:`hsgp_basis`); `rho` defaults to the data half-range (a fixed value -- lengthscale estimation is `hgp_fit`). |
| `boundary` | `float` | — | `1.5` | HSGP marginal parameters (see :func:`hsgp_basis`); `rho` defaults to the data half-range (a fixed value -- lengthscale estimation is `hgp_fit`). |
| `bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | HSGP marginal parameters (see :func:`hsgp_basis`); `rho` defaults to the data half-range (a fixed value -- lengthscale estimation is `hgp_fit`). |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `False` | Sum-to-zero each group's smooth (default `False` -- the group curves carry their own level, the usual factor-smooth; set `True` to pair with an explicit per-group mean). |
| `n_levels` | `Optional[int]` | — | `None` | Number of levels `L` (defaults to `int(group.max()) + 1`); pass it when a level is absent so the block width is stable. |

**Returns** `_FactorGPBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `mrf_smooth` {#mrf_smooth}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:mrf_smooth -->
```python
mrf_smooth(labels: Int[Array, ' n'], neighbours: Float[Array, 'R R'], *, center: bool = True) -> SplineBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:mrf_smooth -->
Build a Markov-random-field smooth (mgcv `bs='mrf'`) over a region
adjacency -- the natural smoother on a parcel / vertex graph.

Each observation carries an integer region `label` in `0 .. R-1`; the
smooth is a per-region effect (design = region indicator) penalised by the
**combinatorial graph Laplacian** `L = D - A` of the region adjacency
(`beta^T L beta = sum_{i~j} (beta_i - beta_j)^2` -- neighbouring regions are
shrunk together).  The penalty is `nitrix.graph.laplacian` -- a direct
substrate reuse.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:mrf_smooth -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `labels` | `Int` | `n` | — | `(n,)` integer region index per observation (`0 .. R-1`). |
| `neighbours` | `Float` | `R` × `R` | — | `(R, R)` region adjacency matrix (non-negative, symmetric; `1` for adjacent regions). |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `True` | Apply the (count-weighted) sum-to-zero constraint (default `True`). |

**Returns** `SplineBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `tensor_product_basis` {#tensor_product_basis}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:tensor_product_basis -->
```python
tensor_product_basis(margins: Tuple[SplineBasis, ...], *, eig_rtol: float = 1e-10) -> TensorBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:tensor_product_basis -->
Build an anisotropic tensor-product interaction smooth from marginals.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:tensor_product_basis -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `margins` | `Tuple[SplineBasis, ...]` | — | — | Two or more :class:`SplineBasis` on the **matched** covariates (same number of rows `n`), each typically sum-to-zero constrained (`center=True`). Mixed kinds are allowed (e.g. a P-spline crossed with a cyclic margin). |
| `eig_rtol` | `float` | — | `1e-10` | Relative tolerance below which a marginal-penalty eigenvalue is floored to exact zero (its unpenalised null space), so the smoothing-parameter trace counts the correct penalty rank. |

**Returns** `TensorBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `re_smooth` {#re_smooth}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:re_smooth -->
```python
re_smooth(g: Int[Array, ' n'], *, by: Optional[Float[Array, ' n']] = None, n_levels: Optional[int] = None) -> REBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:re_smooth -->
Build a random-effect smooth block (mgcv `bs="re"`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:re_smooth -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `g` | `Int` | `n` | — | `(n,)` integer group labels in `0 .. q-1` (the factor levels) -- one per observation. |
| `by` | `Optional[Float[Array, ' n']]` | — | `None` | Optional `(n,)` covariate for a random **slope** `(by \| g)`; `None` gives a random **intercept** `(1 \| g)`. The design is then `one_hot(g) * by[:, None]`. |
| `n_levels` | `Optional[int]` | — | `None` | Number of levels `q` (defaults to `int(g.max()) + 1`). Pass it when some levels are absent from this sample so the block width is stable. |

**Returns** `REBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `by_factor_smooth` {#by_factor_smooth}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:by_factor_smooth -->
```python
by_factor_smooth(x: Float[Array, ' n'], by: Int[Array, ' n'], n_basis: int = 10, *, degree: int = 3, penalty_order: int = 2, bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, center: bool = True, n_levels: Optional[int] = None) -> Tuple[SplineBasis, ...]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:by_factor_smooth -->
By-variable factor smooths `s(x, by=f)` -- one smooth of `x` per level.

mgcv's `s(x, by=f)` for a **factor** `f`: a *separate* penalised smooth
of `x` for each level of `f`, each with its **own** smoothing parameter.
Returns a tuple of `len == n_levels` :class:`SplineBasis` blocks; splat it
into `gam_fit`'s `smooths=` (each block is an independent smooth, so the
Fellner-Schall loop selects a per-level `lambda`).

Construction: a single marginal P-spline of `x` (shared knots / penalty /
centering), then level `l`'s design is that marginal design with the rows
where `f != l` zeroed -- so level `l`'s coefficients load only on its own
observations.  Because the per-level designs have disjoint nonzero rows, the
blocks are orthogonal in `X^T W X` (each level is fit on its own data).
Pair with a parametric factor main effect to carry the per-level mean (the
smooths are sum-to-zero when `center=True`, as in mgcv).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:by_factor_smooth -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `x` | `Float` | `n` | — | `(n,)` covariate. |
| `by` | `Int` | `n` | — | `(n,)` integer factor labels in `0 .. L-1`. |
| `n_basis` | `int` | — | `10` | Marginal P-spline parameters (see :func:`bspline_basis`). |
| `degree` | `int` | — | `3` | Marginal P-spline parameters (see :func:`bspline_basis`). |
| `penalty_order` | `int` | — | `2` | Marginal P-spline parameters (see :func:`bspline_basis`). |
| `bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | Marginal P-spline parameters (see :func:`bspline_basis`). |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `True` | Marginal P-spline parameters (see :func:`bspline_basis`). |
| `n_levels` | `Optional[int]` | — | `None` | Number of levels `L` (defaults to `int(by.max()) + 1`); pass it when a level is absent from this sample so the returned tuple length is stable. |

**Returns** `Tuple[SplineBasis, ...]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `varying_coefficient_smooth` {#varying_coefficient_smooth}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:varying_coefficient_smooth -->
```python
varying_coefficient_smooth(x: Float[Array, ' n'], by: Float[Array, ' n'], n_basis: int = 10, *, degree: int = 3, penalty_order: int = 2, bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, center: bool = True) -> SplineBasis
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:varying_coefficient_smooth -->
Varying-coefficient smooth `s(x, by=z)` for a **continuous** `z`.

mgcv's `s(x, by=z)` with a numeric `by`: the smooth term is
`z * f(x)` -- a coefficient on `z` that varies smoothly with `x`.  The
design is the marginal P-spline of `x` scaled row-wise by `z`; the
penalty is unchanged.  A single :class:`SplineBasis` for `gam_fit`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:varying_coefficient_smooth -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `x` | `Float` | `n` | — | `(n,)` covariate that the coefficient varies over. |
| `by` | `Float` | `n` | — | `(n,)` continuous covariate the smooth multiplies. |
| `n_basis` | `int` | — | `10` | Marginal P-spline parameters (see :func:`bspline_basis`). |
| `degree` | `int` | — | `3` | Marginal P-spline parameters (see :func:`bspline_basis`). |
| `penalty_order` | `int` | — | `2` | Marginal P-spline parameters (see :func:`bspline_basis`). |
| `bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | Marginal P-spline parameters (see :func:`bspline_basis`). |
| `center` | `bool` | — | `True` | Marginal P-spline parameters (see :func:`bspline_basis`). |

**Returns** `SplineBasis`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `spline_design` {#spline_design}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:spline_design -->
```python
spline_design(basis: SplineBasis, x: Float[Array, ' m']) -> Float[Array, 'm k']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:spline_design -->
Re-evaluate a `SplineBasis` design at new covariate values `x`.

Used to render a smooth's partial effect on a fresh grid.  Applies the same
feature map and sum-to-zero constraint as the original construction.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:spline_design -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `basis` | `SplineBasis` | — | — | — |
| `x` | `Float` | `m` | — | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `m` × `k`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `tensor_product_design` {#tensor_product_design}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:tensor_product_design -->
```python
tensor_product_design(basis: TensorBasis, xs: Tuple[Float[Array, ' g'], ...]) -> Float[Array, 'g K']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:tensor_product_design -->
Re-evaluate a `TensorBasis` on a matched covariate grid.

`xs` is one grid per margin (all length `g`); point `t` has covariate
tuple `(xs[0][t], ..., xs[d-1][t])`.  Returns the `(g, K)` tensor design
-- `@` the fitted tensor coefficients renders the interaction surface
along that path.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:tensor_product_design -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `basis` | `TensorBasis` | — | — | — |
| `xs` | `Tuple[Float[Array, ' g'], ...]` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `g` × `K`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
